channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Without US intelligence, Ukraine will struggle to strike targets inside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The U.S. decision to stop sharing military intelligence with Ukraine hobbles its ability to strike and defend against the Russian army, and increases the pressure on it to accept a peace deal being pushed by the Trump administration. Earlier in the week, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU leaders hold emergency talks on defense and Ukraine aid amid waning US security support
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Facing the prospect that the United States might cut them adrift under President Donald Trump, European Union leaders held emergency talks Thursday to explore new ways to beef up their own security and ensure that Ukraine will still be protected.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Murkowski blasts 'callous' DOGE, backs USAID
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski has spoken out against the Trump administration's efforts to gut the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) by slashing jobs and foreign contracts, emphasizing the importance of humanitarian assistance from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Trump hoping to complete southern border wall by 2029: Vance
item.description: Vice President Vance said on Wednesday he thinks President Trump is hoping to finish building a wall along the U.S.-Mexico border by the end of his term in 2029.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: FBI committed to bringing home American hostages held in foreign countries, director says
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel says the bureau will work to “zero out” the population of Americans detained or held hostage in foreign countries.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Poland and Baltic nations welcome Macron's nuclear deterrent proposal
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Poland and Baltic nations welcomed Thursday a proposal by French President Emmanuel Macron to launch talks about using France’s nuclear deterrent to protect the continent from Russian threats, a move Moscow quickly dismissed as “extremely confrontational.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fewer Americans sympathetic toward Israel: Gallup
item.description: Americans are becoming less sympathetic toward Israel in its war against Hamas, the results of a recent survey suggest. A Gallup poll released Thursday shows 46 percent of respondents said they back Israel over the Palestinians when it comes to the "Middle East situation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hamas brushes off Trump's threat and says it will only free hostages in return for a lasting truce
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas on Thursday brushed off President Donald Trump's latest threat and reiterated that it will only free the remaining Israeli hostages in exchange for a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tariffs not the way Trump should slow fentanyl flow: Bolton
item.description: "American importers pay the tariffs and then do everything they can to pass that cost on to American consumers," John Bolton said.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Graham 'OK' with Trump hostage negotiations with Hamas
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) signaled Wednesday evening that he supports President Trump's decision to open direct communication with Hamas on the release of hostages amid the militant group's fragile ceasefire with Israel. “It looks like the administration’s directly negotiating with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: In tense relations with India, Pakistani TV dramas break down barriers that diplomacy often cannot
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — Two Pakistani women sit together on a couch, rehearsing their lines while a director scrutinizes them. Waiting off camera for his scene is the male lead, an actor blessed with “Bachelor” hair and fine bone structure.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Top Hong Kong court overturns convictions of 3 former organizers of Tiananmen vigils
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's top court on Thursday overturned the convictions of three former organizers of an annual vigil in remembrance of the 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown over their refusal to provide information to police, marking a rare victory for the city's pro-democracy activists.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: 5 convicted of plotting to overthrow the German government and kidnap the health minister
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A German court on Thursday convicted five people of plotting to overthrow the government in a far-right coup and kidnap the former health minister. The Koblenz Higher Regional Court sentenced the four ringleaders to prison sentences between five years and nine months to eight years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Romania detains 6 on suspicion of plotting a coup in collusion with Russia
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Six people suspected of colluding with Russia to plot a coup against the Romanian government have been detained, according to police in the European Union and NATO-member country.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and VADIM GHIRDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: France will keep providing military intelligence to Ukraine as the US freezes vital information
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France will keep providing military intelligence to Ukraine after Washington announced it was freezing the sharing of information with Kyiv, French defense Minister Sebastien Lecornu said Thursday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope Francis in stable but 'guarded' condition, according to Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis had a "good night" as he enters his third week of treatment for double pneumonia at Rome’s Gemelli hospital, the Vatican said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Okinawan bone digger searches for remains from one of the fiercest battles of World War II
item.description: ITOMAN, Japan (AP) — Takamatsu Gushiken turns on a headtorch and enters a cave buried in Okinawa's jungle. He gently runs his fingers through the gravel until two pieces of bone emerge. These are from the skulls, he says, of an infant and possibly an adult.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL, HIRO KOMAE and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: UN food agency says it has 2 weeks of supplies in Gaza
item.description: The U.N. World Food Program says it only has enough food supplies in the Gaza Strip to keep public kitchens and bakeries open for less than two weeks, after Israel halted the entry of food, fuel, medicine and other supplies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: One moment, calm waters. The next, a 900-pound dolphin landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 400 kilograms (900 pounds) appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's plan to rebuild Gaza as an alternative to Trump's proposal
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Arab leaders on Tuesday endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip that would allow its roughly 2 million Palestinians to remain, in a counterproposal to U.S. President Donald Trump’s plan to depopulate the territory and redevelop it as a beach destination.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 12 killed, 30 wounded in Pakistan military base attack launched by suicide bombers
item.description: PESHAWAR, Pakistan (AP) — Two suicide bombings breached a wall at a military base in northwestern Pakistan before other attackers stormed the compound and were repelled in violence that killed at least 12 people and wounded 30 others, according to officials and a local hospital.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Serbian lawmakers throw smoke grenades, tear gas in parliament
item.description: Serbian opposition lawmakers let off smoke grenades and tear gas in parliament on Tuesday to protest against the government and support demonstrating students.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis to resume mechanical ventilation as his condition remains stable, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis will resume "non-invasive mechanical ventilation" Tuesday despite his condition remaining stable, the Vatican announced in an evening update.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Philippine fighter jet carrying 2 pilots goes missing during mission against insurgents in southern province
item.description: A Philippine air force fighter jet went missing during a night combat assault in support of ground forces who were battling insurgents in a southern province.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With the Gaza ceasefire in limbo, Israel tries to impose an alternative plan on Hamas
item.description: Israel this week introduced what it said was a new U.S. ceasefire plan — different from the one it agreed to in January — and is trying to force Hamas to accept it by imposing a siege on the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A driver rams a car into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, leaving 2 dead and 11 injured
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver rammed a car into a crowd Monday in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, and authorities said two people were killed and 11 others injured, five of them severely.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump again attacks Zelenskyy with potential peace deal in limbo
item.description: There is a growing chorus of Republicans calling for new Ukrainian leadership following Friday's Oval Office meeting.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire, while key mediator Egypt accused Israel of using “starvation as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope had coughing fit, inhaled vomit and now requires assisted ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered an isolated coughing fit on Friday that resulted in him inhaling vomit and requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying an alarming setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, WGN 9
